task: I wrote a rough note for my teacher about a change in my school day, but it needs to be cleaned up before I send it. Can you help me make it sound clear and school-appropriate?

prefs.short: Wants straightforward answers without beating around the bush
prefs.explanation: He prefers honesty and clear expectations, so indirect or overly softened help would probably feel frustrating. Direct answers fit his routine-minded style and his dislike of unnecessary conflict. He likely responds best when the assistant says what it means plainly.

prefs.short: Likes organized help with clear steps
prefs.explanation: He does well when things are laid out in a predictable order, like a posted schedule or a routine at home. Structured help matches how he thinks about schoolwork and sports, especially when the situation could otherwise feel chaotic. Loose, meandering explanations would likely lose him.

prefs.short: Prefers concise help, not long speeches
prefs.explanation: He seems to want information that gets to the point and can be used right away. Long, rambling responses would add noise rather than clarity, especially when he is already uncomfortable with pressure or confusion. Shorter answers with the important parts first are a better fit.

prefs.short: Likes bullets or checklists over big paragraphs
prefs.explanation: His routine-oriented style suggests he benefits from help that is easy to scan and follow. Bullets, checklists, and step-by-step layouts would match his preference for clear expectations. Dense paragraphs would likely feel harder to sort through.

He seems to dislike chaos and unnecessary conflict, so a steady tone fits him better than hype or emotional intensity. Matter-of-fact language would feel respectful and reassuring. Overly chatty or dramatic help would probably be less comfortable for him.

turns.agent_response: Absolutely — send me your rough note, and I’ll help rewrite it so it sounds clear, polite, and school-appropriate.

per_pref.reason: Direct and plain: "send me your rough note, and I’ll help rewrite it" clearly states what to do without hedging.
It gives a clear next step in an orderly way, though it doesn’t yet provide a full structured breakdown.
Very concise and to the point; it avoids extra explanation and gets straight to the action.
Not bullets/checklists, but the response is short and easy to scan, which partially fits the preference for simple formatting.
Calm, matter-of-fact tone: "Absolutely" and "clear, polite, and school-appropriate" are steady and non-dramatic.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a cleaner, school-appropriate version:

Hi Ms. Carter,

I wanted to let you know that my schedule has changed for tomorrow because I have a doctor’s appointment in the morning. I may miss the beginning of class, but I will arrive as soon as I can. I will make sure to get any missed work and turn it in on time.

Thank you,  
Evan

If you want, I can also make it a little shorter and more casual, or a little more formal.

per_pref.reason: Direct and plain: "here’s a cleaner, school-appropriate version" followed by the rewritten note with no hedging or beating around the bush.
Well organized and easy to follow, with a clear greeting, explanation, and closing. It doesn’t explicitly give step-by-step instructions, but the structure is strong.
Fairly concise and gets to the point quickly. It includes the full revised note plus an optional offer, so it’s not ultra-brief, but it avoids rambling.
Uses a clean, scannable format with separated lines and a ready-to-send draft. It’s not bullets/checklists, but it is easy to read and follow.
Calm, matter-of-fact tone throughout: "Absolutely," "cleaner, school-appropriate version," and a straightforward rewrite without hype or drama.
